front: Ontem eu trabalhei até tarde.
pronunciation: OHN-tayng ew trah-bah-LYAY ah-TEH TAHR-jee.
back: Yesterday I worked until late.
context: Simple past tense of 'trabalhar' (to work) for 'eu' (I).

front: Você falou com ele ontem?
pronunciation: voh-SEH fah-LOW koong EH-lee OHN-tayng?
back: Did you speak with him yesterday?
context: Simple past question using 'falar' (to speak) for 'você' (you).

front: Ele morou em Portugal no ano passado.
pronunciation: EH-lee moh-ROW ayng poor-too-GAW noo AH-noo pah-SAH-doo.
back: He lived in Portugal last year.
context: Simple past tense of 'morar' (to live) for 'ele' (he).

front: Nós estudamos muito para a prova.
pronunciation: nohs ees-too-DAH-moos MOOI-too PAH-rah ah PROH-vah.
back: We studied a lot for the test.
context: Simple past tense of 'estudar' (to study) for 'nós'. Note: it matches the present tense form exactly in spelling.

front: Eles compraram um computador novo.
pronunciation: EH-leys kohm-PRAH-rawng oong kohm-poo-tah-DOHR NOH-voo.
back: They bought a new computer.
context: Simple past tense of 'comprar' (to buy) for 'eles' (they).

front: Eu comi uma pizza deliciosa ontem à noite.
pronunciation: ew koh-MEE OO-mah PEE-tsah deh-lee-syoh-zah OHN-tayng ah NOY-chee.
back: I ate a delicious pizza last night.
context: Simple past tense of the -ER verb 'comer' (to eat) for 'eu' (I).

front: O que você comeu no almoço?
pronunciation: oo kee voh-SEH koh-MEW noo aw-MOH-soo?
back: What did you eat for lunch?
context: Simple past question using 'comer' for 'você' (you).

front: Ela bebeu apenas água na festa.
pronunciation: EH-lah beh-BEW ah-PEH-nahs AH-gwah nah FEHS-tah.
back: She drank only water at the party.
context: Simple past tense of 'beber' (to drink) for 'ela' (she).

front: Nós bebemos muito café hoje cedo.
pronunciation: nohs beh-BEH-moos MOOI-too kah-FEH OH-zhee SEH-doo.
back: We drank a lot of coffee early today.
context: Simple past tense of 'beber' for 'nós'. Note: identical to the present tense form.

front: Eles entenderam a explicação.
pronunciation: EH-leys een-tehn-DEH-rawng ah ees-plee-kah-SOHNG.
back: They understood the explanation.
context: Simple past tense of 'entender' (to understand) for 'eles' (they).

front: Eu escrevi uma mensagem para você.
pronunciation: ew ees-kreh-VEE OO-mah mehng-SAHzhehng PAH-rah voh-SEH.
back: I wrote a message to you.
context: Simple past tense of 'escrever' (to write) for 'eu' (I).

front: Quem escreveu este relatório?
pronunciation: kayng ees-kreh-VEW EHS-chee heh-lah-TOH-ryoo?
back: Who wrote this report?
context: Simple past question using 'escrever' for the third-person singular pronoun.

front: Nós escrevemos o código ontem.
pronunciation: nohs ees-kreh-VEH-moos oo KOH-jee-goo OHN-tayng.
back: We wrote the code yesterday.
context: Simple past tense of 'escrever' for 'nós' (we).

front: Eu abri a porta para ela.
pronunciation: ew ah-BREE ah POHR-tah PAH-rah EH-lah.
back: I opened the door for her.
context: Simple past tense of the -IR verb 'abrir' (to open) for 'eu' (I).

front: Você abriu a janela?
pronunciation: voh-SEH ah-BREW ah zhah-NEH-lah?
back: Did you open the window?
context: Simple past question using 'abrir' for 'você' (you).

front: Eles abriram o presente juntos.
pronunciation: EH-leys ah-BREE-rawng oo preh-ZEHN-chee ZHOON-toos.
back: They opened the gift together.
context: Simple past tense of 'abrir' for 'eles' (they).

front: Eu assisti a um filme no cinema.
pronunciation: ew ah-sees-CHEE ah oong FEEL-mee noo see-NEH-mah.
back: I watched a movie at the cinema.
context: Simple past tense of 'assistir' (to watch) for 'eu' (I).

front: Nós assistimos à aula de JavaScript.
pronunciation: nohs ah-sees-CHEE-moos ah OW-lah jee zhah-vah-SKREEP-chee.
back: We attended the JavaScript class.
context: Simple past tense of 'assistir' (to attend) for 'nós' (we).

front: Eu decidi mudar de profissão.
pronunciation: ew jee-see-DEE moo-DAHR jee proo-fee-SOHNG.
back: I decided to change professions.
context: Simple past tense of 'decidir' (to decide) for 'eu' (I).

front: Eles decidiram o prazo final.
pronunciation: EH-leys jee-see-DEE-rawng oo PRAH-zoo fee-NAW.
back: They decided the final deadline.
context: Simple past tense of 'decidir' for 'eles' (they).

front: Eu dividi a conta com meus amigos.
pronunciation: ew jee-vee-DEE ah KOHN-tah koong mays ah-MEE-goos.
back: I split the bill with my friends.
context: Simple past tense of 'dividir' (to split/share) for 'eu' (I).

front: Eu fui ao supermercado na terça-feira.
pronunciation: ew FOOI ow soo-pehr-mehr-KAH-doo nah TEHR-sah FAY-rah.
back: I went to the supermarket on Tuesday.
context: Simple past tense of the highly irregular verb 'ir' (to go) for 'eu' (I).

front: Você foi ao médico semana passada?
pronunciation: voh-SEH FOOI ow MEH-jee-koo seh-MAH-nah pah-SAH-dah?
back: Did you go to the doctor last week?
context: Simple past question using irregular 'ir' for 'você' (you).

front: Nós fomos à praia no fim de semana.
pronunciation: nohs FOH-moos ah PRY-ah noo feeng jee seh-MAH-nah.
back: We went to the beach over the weekend.
context: Simple past tense of irregular 'ir' for 'nós' (we).

front: Eles foram para casa cedo.
pronunciation: EH-leys FOH-rawng PAH-rah KAH-zah SEH-doo.
back: They went home early.
context: Simple past tense of irregular 'ir' for 'eles' (they).

front: Eu estive em São Paulo no mês passado.
pronunciation: ew ees-CHEE-vee ayng sohng POW-loo noo mays pah-SAH-doo.
back: I was in São Paulo last month.
context: Simple past tense of the irregular verb 'estar' (to be - temporary status) for 'eu' (I).

front: Onde você esteve ontem à tarde?
pronunciation: OHN-jee voh-SEH ees-TEH-vee OHN-tayng ah TAHR-jee?
back: Where were you yesterday afternoon?
context: Simple past question using irregular 'estar' for 'você' (you).

front: Nós estivemos muito ocupados hoje.
pronunciation: nohs ees-chee-VEH-moos MOOI-too oo-koo-PAH-dooz OH-zhee.
back: We were very busy today.
context: Simple past tense of irregular 'estar' for 'nós' (we).

front: Eu fiz um bolo de cenoura.
pronunciation: ew FEES oong BOH-loo jee seh-NOW-rah.
back: I made a carrot cake.
context: Simple past tense of the irregular verb 'fazer' (to make/do) for 'eu' (I).

front: O que você fez no sábado?
pronunciation: oo kee voh-SEH FAYS noo SAH-bah-doo?
back: What did you do on Saturday?
context: Simple past question using irregular 'fazer' for 'você' (you).

front: Eles fizeram todo o dever de casa.
pronunciation: EH-leys fee-ZEH-rawng TOH-doo oo deh-VEHR jee KAH-zah.
back: They did all the homework.
context: Simple past tense of irregular 'fazer' for 'eles' (they).

front: Eu tive uma reunião importante de manhã.
pronunciation: ew CHEE-vee OO-mah hehoo-nee-OHNG eem-pohr-TAHN-chee jee mah-NYAH.
back: I had an important meeting in the morning.
context: Simple past tense of the irregular verb 'ter' (to have) for 'eu' (I).

front: Ela teve muito trabalho esta semana.
pronunciation: EH-lah CHEE-vee MOOI-too trah-BAH-lyoo EHS-tah seh-MAH-nah.
back: She had a lot of work this week.
context: Simple past tense of irregular 'ter' for 'ela' (she).

front: Nós tivemos uma excelente ideia.
pronunciation: nohs chee-VEH-moos OO-mah ay-seh-LEHN-chee ee-DEH-yah.
back: We had an excellent idea.
context: Simple past tense of irregular 'ter' for 'nós' (we).

front: Eles tiveram problemas com o servidor.
pronunciation: EH-leys chee-VEH-rawng proo-BLEH-mahs koong oo sehr-vee-DOHR.
back: They had problems with the server.
context: Simple past tense of irregular 'ter' for 'eles' (they) in a technical operations context.
